description: Thin proxy from OpenClaw skill invocations to Askew's public MCP server (mcp.askew.network). Holds no secrets; reads no environment variables; signs no payments. Any x402 402-challenge envelopes from Askew are passed back through to OpenClaw's runtime so the operator's existing x402 client (e.g. ClawRouter, claw402, or any compliant payer) handles autopayment.

expectedBehaviors.purpose: Forward MCP tools/call requests to Askew's MCP server
expectedBehaviors.justification: The plugin is a thin bridge. OpenClaw skill invocations are translated to MCP tools/call against https://mcp.askew.network/mcp. Only the tool name and arguments are sent; no environment variables, files, or secrets.

expectedBehaviors.purpose: Indirect — Askew's MCP server proxies to x402.askew.network internally; this plugin never calls it directly
expectedBehaviors.justification: Documented for completeness. The plugin only talks to mcp.askew.network. mcp.askew.network may forward calls to x402.askew.network (Askew's x402-paid HTTP API) on the server side; that traffic is server-to-server, not from the operator's machine.

securityNotes: No env-access. The plugin reads zero environment variables and holds no wallet keys.

No payment signing. x402 402-challenge envelopes from Askew flow back through the plugin verbatim; whatever x402-aware runtime invoked the plugin (typically OpenClaw's gateway, or a sibling plugin like ClawRouter) handles autopayment.
Source code is MIT licensed and fully auditable — single-file Node bridge in src/index.js.
Outbound URL is the public Askew MCP server; the operator can override via configSchema.mcpUrl if running a private mirror.
